title: Dark Pools
subtitle: The Rise of the Machine Traders and the Rigging of the U.S. Stock Market
author: Scott Patterson
authorBio: Scott Patterson is an American journalist and author who covered Wall Street and financial regulation for the Wall Street Journal. His reporting on high-frequency trading, dark pools, and market structure has been widely cited. He is also the author of The Quants, about quantitative hedge fund managers, and has appeared on 60 Minutes, CNN, and CNBC.
notableQuotes: The market isn't rigged. It's just becoming inhuman.
Josh Levine built the most successful stock exchange in the world and gave it away for free.
The machines have taken over the stock market.
Dark pools were supposed to make markets safer. Instead they made them invisible.
The rise of the machines on Wall Street is the biggest story in finance since the Great Depression.
